kind._comment: Keyed by the AST node kind string.

left.description:
Declares that an endpoint or message direction is `left`.

- In a channel class, `left` messages are sent by the left endpoint.
- In a process argument, `left` names the role this process plays on the channel.

right.description:
Declares that an endpoint or message direction is `right`.

- In a channel class, `right` messages are sent by the right endpoint.
- In a process argument, `right` names the role this process plays on the channel.

assigned by.description:
Part of a `shared` variable declaration; names the thread index (zero-based) that is responsible for writing the shared variable.

**Example:**

```anvil
shared(#1) result assigned by 0;
```

generate.description:
Compile-time parallel loop unrolling. Instantiates the body expression once for each value of the loop variable, with all instances running concurrently (joined with `;`).

**Example:**

```anvil
generate (i : 0, 3, 1) {
    send eps[i].req(data[i])
}
```

generate_seq.description:
Compile-time sequential loop unrolling. Instantiates the body expression once for each value of the loop variable, with all instances chained sequentially (with `>>`).

**Example:**

```anvil
generate_seq (i : 0, 3, 1) {
    send ep.req(data[i])
}
```

void.description: The unit type. Also expressed as an empty tuple `()`.

recursive.description:
Starts a recursive thread inside a process. Unlike `loop`, the restart point is explicit - the `recurse` expression causes the thread to restart from the beginning. This allows pipelining and conditional restart.

**Example:**

```anvil
recursive {
    let x = recv ep.req >>
    send ep.resp(x) >>
    recurse
}
```

else.description: The else branch of an `if` expression or `try` expression, evaluated when the condition is false or the operation is not immediately ready.

in.description:
Set-membership test. Returns a truthy value if the left operand equals any element in the right-hand set literal.

**Example:**

```anvil
if state in {State::Idle, State::Done} { /* ... */ }
```

with.description:
Struct update syntax. Creates a copy of an existing struct value with one or more fields overridden.

**Example:**

```anvil
Coord::{ base with x = 8'd5 }
```

timingCompletions._comment: Fixed completion entries for the timing annotation heuristic context (inside a channel class message definition after '@'). Split into two sub-sections: 'lifetime' entries appear after the first '@' inside the payload parentheses, and 'sync' entries appear after the closing ')' when adding a sync-mode annotation.

#N.description: **Fixed lifetime**: The message is available for `N` cycles when sent/received.

%s.description: **Relative lifetime**: The message is available for the same number of cycles as `%s`.

%s+N.description: **Relative lifetime**: The message is available for the duration of the lifetime of `%s`, offset by `N` cycles.

eternal.description: **Eternal lifetime**: The message is available indefinitely when sent/received.

#N.description:
**Static synchronization**: The message is sent/received every `N` cycles (beginning on cycle `N`).

**Example:**

```anvil
@#2 // every 2nd cycle
    // (i.e., 2nd, 4th, 6th, ...)
```

#I~N.description:
**Static synchronization**: The message is sent/received every `N` cycles (beginning on cycle `I`).

**Example:**

```anvil
@#1~3 // every 3rd cycle, beginning cycle 1
      // (i.e., 1st, 4th, 7th, ...)
```

dyn.description: **Dynamic synchronization**: The sender and receiver synchronize on messages via handshaking signals.

#%s.description: **Dependent synchronization**: The message is sent/received at the same time as the referenced message `%s`.

#%s+N.description: **Dependent synchronization**: The message is sent/received `N` cycles after the referenced message `%s`.
